title: Number of deaths from cardiovascular diseases
subtitle: Estimated annual number of deaths from [cardiovascular diseases](#dod:cvd).
caption:
Number of deaths from cardiovascular diseases

Estimated annual number of deaths from cardiovascular diseases.

This choropleth world map displays the estimated annual number of deaths from cardiovascular diseases in 2021. The legend uses a color scale ranging from light beige for values near zero to deep red for values exceeding 300,000, with intermediate thresholds marked at 3,000, 10,000, 30,000, and 100,000. The darkest red shades, indicating more than 300,000 deaths, are visible in the United States, Russia, China, India, Brazil, and Indonesia. Several other large nations, including Mexico, Japan, Germany, and the United Kingdom, appear in a slightly lighter but still dark red band, placing them in the range between 100,000 and 300,000. Across Europe, a mix of mid-range and darker shades is present, with countries like France, Italy, and Spain falling into the bands between 30,000 and 100,000. In Africa, most countries are colored in lighter orange or beige tones, suggesting values generally below 30,000, though Nigeria and Ethiopia appear in darker orange shades that sit higher in the mid-range. Australia and Canada are depicted in lighter orange shades, indicating figures between 10,000 and 30,000. Greenland is marked with diagonal hatching, corresponding to the "No data" category in the legend. The visual distribution shows the highest absolute numbers of deaths concentrated in the most populous countries and major economies across North America, Eurasia, and South America, while many regions in Africa and smaller island nations display lighter colors representing lower total counts.
